title: Teaching
text:
University of Sydney, School of Economics (2025–present)

ECON6021 Financial Economics (postgraduate) — Lecturer and Unit Coordinator. Asset pricing theory and the microeconomics of financial markets; curriculum development, assessment design, and unit coordination.
ECON7030 Economics Research Project (postgraduate, Industrial Organisation stream) — Lecturer and Unit Coordinator. Master of Economics capstone unit in which students produce an original research report; I lead the IO stream and coordinate concurrent streams.

title: Teaching
text:

title: Teaching
text:
Teaching Interests
Active learning; extrinsic and intrinsic motivation for learning; use of AI and educational technology; assessment in the presence of AI.

title: Teaching
text:
Teaching Innovation
In ECON6021 I have introduced two technology-led innovations: deployment of the Cogniti AI platform as a Socratic tutor, direct answer tool, and question generator; and BYOD digital examinations using Respondus Lockdown Browser for both in-semester and final assessments.

title: Teaching
text:
Teaching Background
I have seven years of teaching experience across more than fifteen courses at two Australian universities, spanning actuarial science, economics, econometrics, and financial markets.
University of New South Wales (2019–2025)

Co-Lecturer, Probability and Mathematical Statistics (ACTL2131)
Head Tutor, Game Theory and Business Strategy (ECON2112), 2022–2024
Tutor across a broad range of quantitative subjects including: Asset-Liability and Derivative Management (ACTL3182); Statistical Machine Learning for Risk and Actuarial Applications (ACTL5110); Big Data Econometrics (ECON3210); Econometric Theory and Methods (ECON3203); Introductory Econometrics (ECON2206); Quantitative Business Analytics (COMM3501); and Strategic Decision Making (ZZBU5611)
Guest Lecturer, Interdisciplinary Applications of Analytics Techniques (COMM5601)

Software taught across: R, Python, Stata, SAS.
I was nominated for the UNSW Business School Award for Teaching Excellence in Tutoring in 2023.

title: Robert Annabel
text:
Lecturer, School of Economics, University of Sydney
r.w.b.annabel@gmail.com  ·  LinkedIn

I am a Lecturer in the School of Economics at the University of Sydney. I hold a PhD in Economics from UNSW Business School (2025), supervised by Professor Arghya Ghosh, Associate Professor Anthony Asher, Associate Professor Scott Donald, and Professor Hazel Bateman.
My research sits at the intersection of industrial organisation, corporate governance, financial economics and econometric methods. I study how institutional investors — superannuation funds, index funds, and large asset managers — shape the behaviour of the firms they own, with implications for corporate governance, market competition, and sustainability.
I currently teach Financial Economics (ECON6021) and coordinate the Industrial Organisation stream of the Economics Research Project (ECON7030) at the postgraduate level.

title: CV
text:
Download CV (PDF)

A full academic CV is available for download above. It includes academic appointments, education, research output, conference presentations, scholarships and awards, teaching experience, and industry background.

title: Research
text: My research sits at the intersection of industrial organisation, corporate governance, financial economics and econometric methods. Substantive interests include: institutional investors and corporate governance; firm strategy and market structure; econometric theory and causal inference; theories of the firm and firm objectives.

title: Research
text: My research sits at the intersection of industrial organisation, corporate governance, financial economics and econometric methods. Substantive interests include: institutional investors and corporate governance; firm strategy and market structure; econometric theory and causal inference; theories of the firm and firm objectives.

title: Research
text:
Working Papers
[1] Annabel, R., A. Asher, and A. Ghosh (2022). “Company-Level GHG Emissions and an Institutional Investor’s Active Ownership Objective: Long-Term Shareholder, Socially Responsible Investor, Common Owner or Stakeholder-Oriented Shareholder?” SSRN Working Paper 4288009. [SSRN]

title: Research
text:
PhD Thesis
Annabel, R. (2025). Essays on Institutional Investors and Firm Behavior. PhD thesis, School of Economics, UNSW Business School. [DOI: 10.26190/unsworks/31326]
Essay 1 develops theoretical results for identifying active ownership objectives — long-term profit maximisation, common ownership, socially responsible investment, and stakeholder theory — from firm-level greenhouse gas emissions and output.
Essay 2 develops a formal econometric framework for power-based regressions: settings where agents’ relative power mediates group decisions. It provides micro-foundations, conditions for interpreting estimated coefficients, treatment effect bounds, and structural identification strategies.
Essay 3 provides a comprehensive taxonomy of maximisation problems arising from theories of the firm and from theories of investor, manager, and stakeholder objectives, establishing the conceptual infrastructure for empirical identification of investor types.
